summary: Implementation plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function that nightly queries GPT-4o via TRAPI, validates the roster response, and persists it to dedicated Azure Blob Storage using Managed Identity. Covers all infrastructure provisioning, application code, CI/CD pipelines, observability, security controls, testing, and release readiness.
assumptions: TRAPI supports Azure AD Managed Identity bearer token authentication; if not, Key Vault will store a client credential fallback.
TRAPI endpoint and GPT-4o model version will be confirmed by rciapala before APP-01 begins.
Player scope is limited to the 25-man active roster only (no coaching staff, front-office, or IL); validation threshold 24–28.
TRAPI is accessible over the public internet; no VNet integration required for initial delivery.
Consumption Plan is acceptable; no strict cold-start SLA on the 2 AM trigger window.
A single Azure subscription and resource group per environment (staging, prod) is available.
GitHub repository already exists and GitHub Actions is enabled.
Existing Bicep modules (storage, functionapp, rbac, monitoring, alerts) in /infra will be extended rather than replaced.
Python 3.11 runtime and azure-functions v2 SDK are the approved language/framework.
OpenTelemetry SDK for Python is approved for structured logging/metrics emission.
open_questions: Does TRAPI support Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens, or is a client credential required? (Owner: rciapala)
What is the TRAPI base URL and GPT-4o API version/model deployment name to pin?
Should roster scope include coaching staff, front-office, and IL, or active roster only?
Is TRAPI network-restricted (requiring VNet integration + private endpoint)?
Is Consumption Plan cold-start acceptable for the 2 AM UTC SLA, or is Premium Plan required?
What email address(es) should receive execution failure, duration, and data quality alerts?
Is there an existing Log Analytics workspace to reuse, or should a new one be provisioned per environment?
What Azure subscription and tenant IDs should be used for OIDC federated identity setup?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Resolve all blockers, establish infrastructure scaffolding, CI/CD skeleton, and TRAPI auth validation so every subsequent wave can build on working plumbing.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint URL and auth mechanism confirmed by owner
Azure subscription and resource group available per environment
GitHub repository exists with Actions enabled
exit_criteria: TRAPI auth spike completed and auth mechanism documented
Bicep IaC provisions Function App, dedicated storage, Key Vault, App Insights, and RBAC in staging without errors
CD Setup workflow provisions OIDC federated identity successfully
CI workflow runs and passes on push to main

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement all functional requirements — TRAPI client, response validation, blob writer, timer trigger, prompt template, and failure path — with unit test coverage.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint and model version confirmed
Infra provisioned in staging with correct RBAC
exit_criteria: GetAndStoreYankeesRoster function executes end-to-end in staging
Validated roster written to yankees-roster/{date}.json in dedicated storage
Failed responses written to yankees-roster/failed/{date}.json
Unit test suite passes with coverage on all core modules
Timer trigger fires at 0 0 2 * * * UTC in staging

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability controls, observability instrumentation, security hardening, and data quality alerting so the system is production-safe.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
Function deployed and running in staging
exit_criteria: Exponential-backoff retry (3x, 45s timeout) verified under simulated TRAPI failure
Conditional PUT idempotency verified on same-day retrigger
All three alert rules active in Application Insights (failure, duration, data quality)
Structured log events and player_count_returned custom metric emitted and queryable
No secrets or connection strings in source code or workflow files
Key Vault references validated in App Settings

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end in staging, promote to production via slot swap, confirm monitoring is active, and complete documentation.
entry_criteria: All unit and integration tests passing
exit_criteria: Smoke test passing in production after slot swap
Nightly execution verified in production (at least one successful run)
All success criteria in spec.md checked off
README complete with local dev, TRAPI auth, and blob naming instructions
Monitoring and alerting active in production

tasks.title: Resolve TRAPI auth mechanism and endpoint details
tasks.description: Confirm with the TRAPI team whether Managed Identity bearer tokens are supported, obtain the base URL, API version, and GPT-4o deployment name. Document the resolution in spec.md under Open Questions 1 and 2, replacing the TBD placeholders with confirmed values. If Managed Identity is unsupported, add a Key Vault secret design note for the fallback credential.
outputs: Updated spec.md with confirmed TRAPI auth mechanism, endpoint URL, and model version
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI auth mechanism (Managed Identity or Key Vault credential) documented in spec.md
TRAPI base URL and GPT-4o model deployment name confirmed and recorded
If Key Vault fallback required, Key Vault secret schema defined
risks: Blocked on external TRAPI team response; may delay APP-01 and INF-01

tasks.title: Clarify player scope and validation thresholds
tasks.description: Confirm whether the roster scope is active roster only (24–28) or includes coaching staff, injured list, and front-office. Update spec.md Requirement 1 with the confirmed scope and adjust the prompt template acceptance criteria and validation thresholds accordingly.
outputs: Updated spec.md with confirmed player scope and validation threshold range
acceptance_criteria: Player scope explicitly stated in spec.md (active roster only or broader)
Validation threshold range (min/max player count) confirmed and updated in spec.md
Prompt template acceptance criteria updated to reflect scope
risks: Ambiguous scope leads to incorrect prompt template and validation logic

tasks.title: Extend Bicep IaC for Key Vault and all RBAC assignments
tasks.description: Add a Key Vault Bicep module (zone-redundant, Key Vault Secrets User RBAC for Function App MI) and ensure all required role assignments are wired in main.bicep: Storage Blob Data Contributor scoped to the yankees-roster container, Key Vault Secrets User, and Queue Data Contributor (host storage). Verify dedicated storage account uses Cool access tier and soft-delete 7-day retention.
acceptance_criteria: Key Vault provisioned zone-redundant with Key Vault Secrets User assigned to Function App MI
Storage Blob Data Contributor scoped to yankees-roster container (not account-level)
Dedicated storage account has Cool access tier and 7-day soft-delete enabled
allowSharedKeyAccess:false on dedicated storage account
Bicep deployment completes without errors in staging resource group
No hardcoded secrets in any Bicep template
risks: RBAC propagation delay can cause Function App startup failures; allow 5-min post-deploy wait in CD workflow

tasks.title: Add staging deployment slot to Function App Bicep
tasks.description: Add a staging slot resource to the Function App Bicep module to support the CD Promote slot-swap workflow. Ensure the staging slot has its own App Settings referencing the same Key Vault and dedicated storage account, and that slot-sticky settings are correctly configured.
outputs: Updated infra/modules/functionapp.bicep with staging slot resource
acceptance_criteria: Staging slot provisioned under the Function App
Staging slot App Settings include APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING and AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName
Slot-sticky settings (e.g., environment name) correctly marked
Slot swap does not transfer secrets or slot-sticky config
risks: Consumption Plan slots have limitations; verify slot swap is supported on Consumption SKU

tasks.title: Implement CD Setup workflow with OIDC federated identity
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/cd-setup.yml that provisions a User-Assigned Managed Identity (or configures the existing system-assigned MI) with federated OIDC credentials for GitHub Actions, assigns it Contributor/required roles on the subscription, and outputs the CLIENT_ID and TENANT_ID as GitHub environment secrets. No client secrets or passwords stored.
acceptance_criteria: cd-setup.yml workflow triggers manually and completes without error
OIDC federated credential created for GitHub repo/environment
No client secrets or passwords stored in workflow or repository
GitHub environment variables AZURE_CLIENT_ID and AZURE_TENANT_ID set after workflow run
README.md updated with OIDC setup instructions
risks: Requires elevated Azure AD permissions to create federated credentials; may need AAD admin

tasks.title: Implement CI workflow for unit tests
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml triggered on push to main (disabled by default via workflow_dispatch condition or manual enable). Workflow installs Python 3.11, installs requirements.txt, and runs pytest with coverage reporting. Fails on test failure.
acceptance_criteria: ci.yml triggers on push to main and runs all pytest tests
Workflow is disabled by default (workflow_dispatch or manual enable documented)
Coverage report output in CI logs
Workflow fails if any test fails
risks: Workflow enabled prematurely before tests exist will cause false failures

tasks.title: Implement CD Infra workflow
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/cd-infra.yml that triggers manually with an environment parameter, authenticates via OIDC, runs az deployment group create with the Bicep main.bicep, then executes an infra validation test (e.g., verify storage container exists, Key Vault accessible, Function App running).
acceptance_criteria: cd-infra.yml triggers manually with environment input parameter
Authenticates to Azure via OIDC (no stored secrets)
Bicep deployment completes successfully in target environment
Infra validation test confirms storage container, Key Vault, and Function App are reachable
Workflow fails if Bicep deployment or validation test fails
risks: OIDC role assignments must be complete before this workflow can run

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client with retry and timeout
tasks.description: Implement or extend src/trapi_client.py to support a 45-second HTTP timeout and 3-retry exponential-backoff policy for TRAPI GPT-4o calls. Accept the pinned model version and prompt as parameters. Authenticate via DefaultAzureCredential with the confirmed TRAPI auth scope. Return the raw response body or raise a typed TRAPIException on exhausted retries.
inputs: DES-01 confirmed TRAPI endpoint and auth scope
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI HTTP call uses 45-second timeout
3 retries with exponential backoff on transient failures (5xx, timeout)
Pinned GPT-4o model version passed as parameter
DefaultAzureCredential used; no hardcoded credentials
TRAPIException raised after retries exhausted
Unit tests mock HTTP calls and verify retry count, timeout, and exception behavior
risks: Blocked on DES-01 TRAPI endpoint/auth confirmation

tasks.title: Implement prompt template loading and response schema validation
tasks.description: Create prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt with the pinned GPT-4o prompt. Implement src/roster_validator.py to validate the TRAPI JSON response against the expected schema (players[].name, position, jersey_number) and player count range (24–28 or confirmed threshold). Return a typed ValidationResult with player list or validation errors.
inputs: spec.md Requirement 1 (prompt template, schema, player count)
acceptance_criteria: prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt committed to source control with pinned model version reference
Validator rejects responses missing name, position, or jersey_number fields
Validator rejects player counts outside confirmed threshold range
Validator confirms presence of Mattingly, Winfield, and Henderson
Unit tests cover schema pass, schema fail, count too low, count too high, and missing known players
ValidationResult type returned (not raw dict)
risks: GPT-4o non-determinism may produce varying player counts; thresholds must be confirmed before finalizing validator

tasks.title: Implement blob writer with conditional PUT and failure path
tasks.description: Implement or extend src/blob_writer.py to write validated roster JSON to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json using a conditional PUT (If-None-Match: *) to prevent duplicate writes. On validation failure, write the raw response to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json. Authenticate via DefaultAzureCredential (Managed Identity).
inputs: spec.md Requirement 2 (blob naming, conditional PUT, failure path)
acceptance_criteria: Successful write goes to yankees-roster/{UTC_DATE}.json in dedicated storage account
Conditional PUT (If-None-Match: *) returns without error on duplicate attempt
Failed validation writes to yankees-roster/failed/{UTC_DATE}.json
DefaultAzureCredential used; no connection strings or shared keys
Unit tests cover successful write, duplicate write (409 handled), and failure path write
risks: If-None-Match behavior on Azure Blob may differ from expectation on overwrite; verify SDK support

tasks.title: Implement GetAndStoreYankeesRoster Timer Trigger function
tasks.description: Implement or update src/function_app.py with the GetAndStoreYankeesRoster Timer Trigger function using CRON schedule 0 0 2 * * * UTC, useMonitor: true, and maxConcurrentCalls: 1. Orchestrates: load prompt → call TRAPI client → validate response → write blob (success or failure path) → emit structured log events and player_count_returned custom metric.
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger schedule is 0 0 2 * * * UTC with useMonitor: true
host.json sets functionTimeout to 120 seconds and maxConcurrentCalls to 1
Function emits function_started, trapi_request_sent, trapi_response_received, blob_write_succeeded, function_completed log events
player_count_returned custom metric emitted on each successful run
On failure: raw response written to failed/ prefix; alert-triggering exception raised
Unit tests mock all dependencies and verify orchestration logic and log event emission
risks: OpenTelemetry SDK integration with Azure Functions v2 may require additional configuration

tasks.title: Extend Application Insights alerts for duration and data quality
tasks.description: Extend infra/modules/alerts.bicep to add two additional alert rules: (1) duration alert firing when function execution duration > 90 seconds, (2) data quality alert on custom metric player_count_returned < 24 or > 40. All alerts notify rciapala via the existing email Action Group.
acceptance_criteria: Duration alert fires when execution duration > 90s in Application Insights
Data quality alert fires when player_count_returned < 24 or > 40
Execution failure alert fires when failure count > 0 in 1-hour window
All alerts route to rciapala email via Action Group
Alert rules provisioned via Bicep without manual portal configuration
risks: Custom metric player_count_returned must be emitted by the function before the alert can be tested

tasks.title: Audit and enforce no-secrets posture across repo and workflows
tasks.description: Scan all source files, Bicep templates, workflow files, and local.settings.json.example for any hardcoded secrets, connection strings, or API keys. Ensure local.settings.json is in .gitignore. Verify all sensitive App Settings use Key Vault references (@Microsoft.KeyVault(...)). Add a git-secrets or trufflehog pre-commit hook or CI step.
outputs: CI secret-scan step in ci.yml or separate security scan workflow
acceptance_criteria: No secrets, connection strings, or API keys in any committed file
local.settings.json is in .gitignore
All sensitive App Settings use Key Vault references in Bicep
Secret scan step runs in CI and fails on detected secrets
TRAPI auth scope (if used as App Setting) stored as Key Vault reference
risks: Historical commits may contain secrets; git history scan recommended

tasks.title: Write integration test for end-to-end roster retrieval and blob output
tasks.description: Write an integration test (tests/test_integration.py) that invokes the function against the staging environment, waits for blob output, and asserts that Mattingly, Winfield, and Henderson appear in the roster JSON. Parameterize with staging storage account name and container. Intended to run as the smoke test in CD App workflow.
inputs: spec.md Success Criteria (integration test, known players)
acceptance_criteria: Integration test reads blob from yankees-roster/{today_utc}.json in staging storage
Asserts Mattingly, Winfield, and Henderson present in players array
Asserts player count is within configured threshold range
Test can be triggered from CI/CD workflow as smoke test
Test is skipped (not failed) if blob does not yet exist for today (early run)
risks: Test depends on nightly trigger having fired; may need manual trigger or direct function invocation for daytime test runs

tasks.title: Implement CD App and CD Promote workflows
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/cd-app.yml (manual trigger, environment parameter, deploys function code via az functionapp deployment, runs smoke test) and .github/workflows/cd-promote.yml (manual trigger, source/target environment parameters defaulting to stage→prod, performs az functionapp deployment slot swap).
inputs: spec.md CI/CD section (CD App, CD Promote)
acceptance_criteria: cd-app.yml deploys function code to specified environment using OIDC (no stored secrets)
cd-app.yml runs smoke test after deployment and fails workflow on smoke test failure
cd-promote.yml performs slot swap from source (default: stage) to target (default: prod)
cd-promote.yml requires manual approval or confirmation step before swap
No client secrets or passwords in any workflow file
risks: Slot swap on Consumption Plan must be verified as supported; fallback is direct redeploy to prod

tasks.title: Write README with local dev, TRAPI auth, blob naming, and CI/CD setup
tasks.description: Create or update README.md with: local dev setup (venv, local.settings.json.example usage, func start), TRAPI auth instructions (Managed Identity scope or Key Vault secret path), blob naming convention, how to run CI/CD workflows, and how to set up the OIDC federated identity for the CD managed identity.
acceptance_criteria: README includes local dev setup steps (venv, pip install, local.settings.json)
README documents TRAPI auth mechanism and required environment variables
README documents blob naming convention (yankees-roster/{UTC_DATE}.json)
README includes step-by-step instructions for running each GitHub Actions workflow
README includes instructions for setting up OIDC federated identity
risks: Documentation is blocked on DES-01 TRAPI auth confirmation; draft can proceed with TBD placeholder

tasks.title: Execute end-to-end staging validation and production deployment
tasks.description: Run the full release sequence: trigger CD Infra for production, run CD App to staging, verify nightly timer fires in staging (or manual trigger), run integration smoke test, then run CD Promote to swap staging→production. Verify production smoke test passes and monitoring alerts are active.
inputs: REL-04 CD App and CD Promote workflows
outputs: Smoke test pass evidence (CI/CD run link)
Alert rule confirmation screenshot or ARM query result
acceptance_criteria: CD Infra workflow completes for production environment without errors
CD App deploys to staging and smoke test passes
CD Promote slot swap completes and production smoke test passes
At least one successful nightly execution observed in production Application Insights
All three alert rules (failure, duration, data quality) confirmed active in production
All spec.md success criteria checkboxes verified and documented
risks: Cold-start on Consumption Plan may cause first production execution to exceed 60s
Alert rules for player_count_returned require at least one function execution to emit the custom metric before alerting can be validated
